Home How It Works Materials & Finishes Pricing / Quote Use Cases FAQ Contact Upload Your File

Fast, practical 3D printing

Custom 3D printed parts for one-offs, prototypes, and small batches.

Upload a file and request a quote for replacement parts, functional prints, prototype housings, hobby projects, and short-run custom jobs. Every request is reviewed by a real person before printing starts.

Upload Your File Request a Quote

Supported files: STL, STEP, OBJ, 3MF

STLSTEPOBJ3MF
Human-reviewed quotes
One-off and short-run friendly
Local pickup or shipping
Practical material guidance

How to get started

  1. 1. Upload your model or describe the part

    Send an STL, STEP, OBJ, or 3MF file. No model yet? A sketch or reference photo is still useful.

  2. 2. Receive a quote and material guidance

    The job is reviewed for size, strength, finish expectations, and turnaround.

  3. 3. Approve the job and we print it

    Pickup or shipping can be arranged once the part passes a final check.

Best for

Replacement parts, prototypes, small batch components, and custom one-offs.

Response time

Typical quote replies within 1 business day

How It Works

A simple quote-to-print process.

This service is designed for customers who want a straightforward answer, not a complicated manufacturing portal.

01

Upload your file or send the job details

Share an STL, STEP, OBJ, or 3MF file. If you do not have a model yet, send a sketch, reference photo, or a description of the part you need.

02

Receive a quote and material recommendation

Every job is reviewed by a real person. You will get pricing, lead time, and help choosing the right material or finish for the part.

03

We print, inspect, and arrange delivery or pickup

Once approved, the job moves into print prep and production. Small parts can often move quickly, with pickup or shipping arranged when finished.

What We Print

Useful parts for real jobs.

From broken part replacements to prototype housings, the focus is on custom prints that solve a practical problem.

Replacement parts

Clips, brackets, covers, knobs, spacers, and hard-to-find parts for home, workshop, or equipment repairs.

Prototypes

Simple prototype housings, jigs, fixtures, and early-stage product parts for fast iteration.

Hobby and maker projects

Project enclosures, adapters, mounts, and custom components for makers who need parts without buying a printer.

Custom one-offs

Need a single part made for a specific job? One-off prints are welcome, even if the job is unusual.

Small batch parts

Short runs for test units, internal tools, or low-volume business needs where injection molding does not make sense.

Materials Preview

Common materials for functional and detail-focused parts.

Material choice affects strength, finish, cost, and how the part will behave in real use.

View All Materials

PLA

Most affordable

Easy to print, clean-looking, and budget-friendly for many everyday parts and visual models.

Best for
Concept models, light-duty brackets, enclosures, display parts
Durability
Moderate durability

PETG

Affordable mid-range

A strong all-around material with better toughness and moisture resistance than PLA.

Best for
Functional parts, workshop accessories, housings, utility brackets
Durability
Good durability

ABS

Mid-range

More heat-tolerant and suitable for tougher functional parts when the geometry and application are a good fit.

Best for
Functional prototypes, shop parts, warmer environments
Durability
Good impact and heat resistance

Resin

Higher cost

Higher-detail prints for small parts, smooth surfaces, and presentation pieces.

Best for
Miniatures, fine-detail components, display models, cosmetic prototypes
Durability
Varies by resin type

Why Choose Us

Small-shop service without small-shop uncertainty.

The advantage of a personal print service is that you can ask questions, get practical guidance, and order low-volume jobs without a factory-scale workflow.

Human review before printing

You are not sending files into a black box. Every quote is reviewed for printability, material fit, and practical next steps.

Fast turnaround for straightforward jobs

Small parts and simple repeat jobs can often move quickly. If a deadline matters, include it in the quote request.

Clear communication

If a model needs adjustment, a part looks weak, or a better material is available, you will hear about it before printing starts.

Friendly to one-offs and low volume

This service is built for the kind of work that bigger factories often ignore: single parts, short runs, and practical custom jobs.

Personal local service

Local pickup can be arranged, and questions are answered by the person actually reviewing and printing the job.

Help for non-technical customers

If you are new to 3D printing, you can still get help. A clear description or reference photo is often enough to start the conversation.

Customer Trust

Built to feel clear, reliable, and easy to work with.

Use real customer feedback here before launch. The sample cards below are placeholders that show the intended tone and layout.

Quote-first workflow

Upload a file, describe the job, and get a manual quote instead of a misleading fake instant price.

Designed for practical jobs

From broken part replacements to quick prototype runs, the site is built for real-world small-batch work.

Support if your file is not perfect

If a wall looks too thin, a hole needs resizing, or a better orientation is possible, those issues can be flagged before printing.

"I needed a replacement clip for a kitchen appliance that was no longer sold anywhere. The part fit well and the turnaround was much faster than I expected."

Sample review from a repair customer

"We used Forgefront 3D for a simple prototype housing before committing to a larger manufacturing step. The communication was clear and the print was ready to test."

Sample review from an early-stage product team

"I sent over a hobby project with almost no technical notes. They helped choose material, checked the model, and made the process easy."

Sample review from a first-time customer

FAQ

Common questions before you upload a file.

A few fast answers for first-time customers and repeat buyers.

View Full FAQ
What file types do you accept? +

The preferred upload formats are STL, STEP, OBJ, and 3MF. If your CAD software exports another format, get in touch first and we can confirm whether it will work.

How long does printing take? +

Lead time depends on part size, quantity, material, and current queue. Straightforward small parts can often move quickly, while larger or more detailed jobs may take longer. If you have a deadline, include it with the quote request.

How much does 3D printing cost? +

Pricing depends on material, print time, part size, quantity, finish requirements, and urgency. Uploading your file is the fastest way to get an accurate quote.

What if I do not have a 3D file? +

You can still reach out. A sketch, broken part, photo, or simple dimensions may be enough to discuss whether the job is possible and what the next step should be.

Can you help fix or review a model? +

Basic file review is part of the quoting process. If a model has obvious printability issues or needs a small adjustment, that can be discussed before the job moves forward.

What materials do you offer? +

Common options include PLA, PETG, ABS, and resin, depending on the part requirements and current stock. If you need something specific, ask during the quote request.

Ready To Start?

Need a part printed? Upload your file and request a quote.

Straightforward jobs can move quickly, and non-technical customers are welcome. Send the file if you have it, or send a clear description if you do not.

Upload Your File Contact Us For A Custom Job

Fast, practical 3D printing for replacement parts, prototypes, custom jobs, and small batches. Built for customers who want clear answers and a real quote without enterprise complexity.
